protocol_definition.message_flow: Agents negotiate roles, execute an allocation, adapt to gaps, and Writer finalizes.
protocol_definition.visibility: Agents see shared negotiation history and relevant intermediate work.
protocol_definition.termination: Stop after adaptation and Writer output or when the interaction budget is reached.

final_call.expected_action: Produce a self-contained final deliverable from the adapted work. Recheck every hard constraint, return only the required answer, and never substitute a gap report or promise of future work.

intermediate_messages.content:
1) Brief Market Overview
Slack and Microsoft Teams are leading collaboration platforms used by small and large teams for messaging, calls, and app integrations. Slack positions itself as an AI-enabled work platform focused on sustaining conversations, file sharing, and a rich marketplace of third-party apps. Microsoft Teams is integrated tightly with Microsoft 365, emphasizing persistent chat, real-time meetings, file collaboration via SharePoint/OneDrive, and deep Office apps integration. For a small remote company needing daily messaging, file sharing, video meetings, and basic integrations, both offer core capabilities, but their ecosystems and integration approaches differ.

2) Product Comparison Table
- Product: Slack
  - Target Users: Teams and organizations seeking a centralized, conversation-first workspace with a broad app marketplace; teams of varying sizes including small businesses.
  - Core Communication Features: Channel-based messaging, direct messages, audio/video huddles, file sharing, searchable conversations.
  - Collaboration and Integrations: Extensive Apps & Integrations via Slack App Directory; Workflow Builder to automate tasks; Slackbot AI features; external collaboration through Slack Connect.
  - Main Strength: Rich, centralized conversation lens with a large ecosystem of integrations and AI-assisted productivity features (Slackbot, memory, prompts) that surface data from across tools.
  - Main Limitation: The breadth of integrations can be overwhelming; pricing and plan differences may affect access to advanced features (noting that featured AI and enterprise-grade controls exist in higher tiers).

- Product: Microsoft Teams
  - Target Users: Organizations already using Microsoft 365; teams seeking seamless Office/Outlook/SharePoint integration with chat, meetings, and files in a single suite.
  - Core Communication Features: Persistent chat, video meetings with meeting options, calls, and presence; integrated with Office apps.
  - Collaboration and Integrations: Deep integration with SharePoint/OneDrive, Office apps (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), and Microsoft 365 services; supports third-party apps via Teams apps and connectors.
  - Main Strength: End-to-end productivity integration within the Microsoft 365 ecosystem, especially strong for document collaboration and meeting workflows.
  - Main Limitation: Can be more complex to configure for non-Microsoft workflows; user experience and performance vary with environment and plan.

3) Recommendation
Recommended: Microsoft Teams for a small remote company with daily messaging, file sharing, video meetings, and basic integrations with workplace tools. Evidence supporting Teams’ fit includes:
- Core communication and collaboration alignment with Office apps and SharePoint/OneDrive for file sharing and simultaneous editing.
- Strong meeting capability tightly integrated with calendar and Office apps, benefiting teams already using Microsoft 365.
- Enterprise-grade governance and security controls integrated across Microsoft 365 services, aiding small businesses growing into more formal IT practices.

This recommendation is grounded in Microsoft’s positioning of Teams as the group chat and collaboration hub within Microsoft 365, delivering persistent chat, meetings, and file collaboration in a single suite, which is advantageous for small remote teams leveraging Office tools and standard workplace workflows. Slack’s strengths in a broad app ecosystem are clear, but for a small remote company prioritizing streamlined Office integration, Teams provides a cohesive, familiar environment with straightforward file collaboration and meeting workflows.

4) Sources
- Slack: Slack product pages and features (Channels, Huddles, file sharing, Slack Apps & Integrations, Slackbot AI features, Slack Connect) from Slack’s official site. Retrieved via Slack’s features overview and AI work platform pages. https://slack.com and https://slack.com/features (retrieval date 2026-07-22).
- Microsoft Teams: Microsoft/Teams product overview highlighting chat, meetings, file collaboration with Office 365 integration and Teams for work pages. Retrieved from official Microsoft Teams product pages and Microsoft 365/Teams ecosystem guidance (retrieval date 2026-07-22). https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-teams/group-chat-software and https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-teams/teams-for-work

Excellent question. Slack is a new way for your entire company to work. Instead of email, Slack is a faster, better-organized, and more secure platform — all your communication is organized into channels that are easy to create, join, and search. When there’s a channel for everything going on at your company, everyone knows exactly where to go to get work done. Slack also brings AI into the flow of work, helping you throughout your workday, making it faster and easier to find and act on the information you need. And unlike email, Slack integrates with all the other systems in your tech stack to become the single OS for all your work. Slack puts everything and everyone you need to get work done in one single place, so no more context switching, data silos, or lost intel. For more reading on the topic, we recommend checking out our Resources Library . How do channels in Slack drive productivity? Work in Slack starts with channels. By creating a channel for all your projects, teams, offices, and departments — everything you’re doing at work — you create a space for every conversation to happen. And because channels are easy to join and create, Slack can adapt to meet changing needs. If someone new joins a project, simply add them to the channel and they can scroll up to read through old conversations. When it’s time to start something new, create a new channel and invite the right people. To learn more, read up on how to collaborate effectively in channels . How does Slack AI protect the security of your data? Slack AI runs on Slack’s trusted infrastructure, and upholds the security practices and compliance standards that customers expect from Slack itself. Slack does not share customer data with LLM (large language model) providers and does not use customer data to train LLMs. Our LLMs are hosted directly within Slack’s AWS virtual private cloud, ensuring that your data remains in-house. Learn more here . Is Slack secure? Yes — you can securely discuss confidential information in Slack. Slack offers multiple ways to ensure that your information, conversations, and files stay safe. Slack delivers enterprise-grade security at every layer, adhering to multiple compliance certifications, including SOC 2, SOC 3, ISO/IEC 27001, and more. Slack is GDPR-compliant and can be configured for HIPAA and FINRA compliance. It is FedRAMP Moderate authorized. In addition, Slack offers advanced security features, like Enterprise Key Management, that allow admins fine-grained control over data encryption. You can also integrate your own security tools with Slack to get an instant notification if a threat is detected. Learn more about Slack’s comprehensive security program here . Is Slack more secure than e-mail? Yes! Unlike email, Slack is not susceptible to spam or phishing, which causes 90% of data breaches. Your Slack handle cannot be sold to advertisers or put on a mailing list, and you will only ever receive Slack messages from other people inside your organization, or from trusted partners using Slack Connect . You may get notifications from apps integrated with your workspace, such as Asana, Google Docs, or Jira. Slack offers enterprise-grade data protection and privacy. Granular controls allow admins to customize security for each user, so no one sees anything they shouldn’t. Learn more about how Slack can securely replace email inside your company. What is Slack Connect? Slack Connect is a more secure and productive way for organizations to communicate together. It lets you move all the conversations with your external partners, clients, vendors, and others into Slack, replacing email and fostering collaboration. Slack’s enterprise-grade security features and compliance standards, like Enterprise Key Management, extend to Slack Connect. Learn more about Slack Connect here . esc Slack is where work happens. 1) Brief Market Overview
Slack and Microsoft Teams are leading collaboration platforms used by small and large teams for messaging, calls, and app integrations. Slack positions itself as an AI-enabled work platform focused on sustaining conversations, file sharing, and a rich marketplace of third-party apps. Microsoft Teams is integrated tightly with Microsoft 365, emphasizing persistent chat, real-time meetings, file collaboration via SharePoint/OneDrive, and deep Office apps integration. For a small remote company needing daily messaging, file sharing, video meetings, and basic integrations, both offer core capabilities, but their ecosystems and integration approaches differ.

2) Product Comparison Table
- Product: Slack
  - Target Users: Teams and organizations seeking a centralized, conversation-first workspace with a broad app marketplace; teams of varying sizes including small businesses.
  - Core Communication Features: Channel-based messaging, direct messages, audio/video huddles, file sharing, searchable conversations.
  - Collaboration and Integrations: Extensive Apps & Integrations via Slack App Directory; Workflow Builder to automate tasks; Slackbot AI features; external collaboration through Slack Connect.
  - Main Strength: Rich, centralized conversation lens with a large ecosystem of integrations and AI-assisted productivity features (Slackbot, memory, prompts) that surface data from across tools.
  - Main Limitation: The breadth of integrations can be overwhelming; pricing and plan differences may affect access to advanced features (noting that featured AI and enterprise-grade controls exist in higher tiers).

- Product: Microsoft Teams
  - Target Users: Organizations already using Microsoft 365; teams seeking seamless Office/Outlook/SharePoint integration with chat, meetings, and files in a single suite.
  - Core Communication Features: Persistent chat, video meetings with meeting options, calls, and presence; integrated with Office apps.
  - Collaboration and Integrations: Deep integration with SharePoint/OneDrive, Office apps (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), and Microsoft 365 services; supports third-party apps via Teams apps and connectors.
  - Main Strength: End-to-end productivity integration within the Microsoft 365 ecosystem, especially strong for document collaboration and meeting workflows.
  - Main Limitation: Can be more complex to configure for non-Microsoft workflows; user experience and performance vary with environment and plan.

3) Recommendation
Recommended: Microsoft Teams for a small remote company with daily messaging, file sharing, video meetings, and basic integrations with workplace tools. Evidence supporting Teams’ fit includes:
- Core communication and collaboration alignment with Office apps and SharePoint/OneDrive for file sharing and simultaneous editing.
- Strong meeting capability tightly integrated with calendar and Office apps, benefiting teams already using Microsoft 365.
- Enterprise-grade governance and security controls integrated across Microsoft 365 services, aiding small businesses growing into more formal IT practices.

This recommendation is grounded in Microsoft’s positioning of Teams as the group chat and collaboration hub within Microsoft 365, delivering persistent chat, meetings, and file collaboration in a single suite, which is advantageous for small remote teams leveraging Office tools and standard workplace workflows. Slack’s strengths in a broad app ecosystem are clear, but for a small remote company prioritizing streamlined Office integration, Teams provides a cohesive, familiar environment with straightforward file collaboration and meeting workflows.
